Grade vector from a gradepath fit (base-generic synonym for get_grades)

Source: R/accessors.R
coef.gp_fit.Rd

coef() method for gp_fit: the "fitted summary" of a gradepath fit is its per-unit grading, so coef(fit) returns the same named integer grade vector as get_grades(). Provided because coef() is the base generic an R user reaches for first (GP-DEC-13-A, Chapter 13 patterns; Chapter 14 ux). Grade labels are integers in {1, ..., n} and carry no ranking-superiority statement of any kind.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'gp_fit'
coef(object, ...)

Arguments

object

A gp_fit object.

...

Unused; accepted for S3 method extensibility. No arguments are forwarded; supplying any has no effect.

Value

A named integer vector of grades, identical to get_grades(object).

See also

get_grades(), as.data.frame.gp_fit()

Other gradepath-accessors: as.data.frame.gp_fit(), get_control(), get_grades(), get_pairwise(), get_posterior(), get_prior(), get_report_card()

Examples

# Instant: coef() is the grade vector of the bundled tiny fit.
fit <- readRDS(system.file("extdata/examples/tiny_fit.rds", package = "gradepath"))
head(coef(fit))        # == head(get_grades(fit))
#> firm01 firm02 firm03 firm04 firm05 firm06 
#>      1      2      1      1      1      2
